title: Multi-Output ATX PSU
description: A multi-output flyback converter modelling the secondary side of an ATX computer power supply: 12 V/18 A (main), 5 V/30 A (logic), and 3.3 V/25 A (CPU/DRAM) from a 300–375 V PFC bus.
design_notes: The 12 V rail is selected as the regulated primary output (feedback loop closes on it). The 5 V and 3.3 V rails are derived via additional secondary windings and are cross-regulated — meaning their output voltages vary with load balance between rails. The cross-regulation estimate (shown in secondary output results) is typically ±3–5 % under a ±50 % primary load swing and can be improved with a magnetic amplifier (magamp) post-regulator on the 5 V winding. Modern ATX12V v2.x supplies derive the 3.3 V rail from a separate buck post-regulator on the 5 V output, eliminating the cross-regulation problem but adding a component. The 300–375 V input represents the output of the front-end PFC boost stage; the PFC stage is not modelled here. Total power across all outputs: 12×18 + 5×30 + 3.3×25 ≈ 582 W.
source: Erickson & Maksimovic §13.2 (multi-output); ATX12V PSU Design Guide v2.52
